operating_state: Total revenue increased to $6.0 billion for Q2 2021 from $2.9 billion in Q2 2020, driven by significantly higher commodity prices with worldwide oil realizations averaging $64.18 versus $23.14 in the prior year. Production volumes declined to 1,199 Mboe/d from 1,386 Mboe/d primarily due to reduced capital investment in the Permian and DJ Basins. Chemical operations benefited from improved prices across most product lines while midstream captured higher sulfur prices and optimized natural gas transportation.
cost_structure: Oil and gas operating expenses decreased due to lower production volumes while transportation and gathering costs fell with reduced domestic volumes. DD&A expense increased due to higher depletion rates from lower 2020 reserve estimates, though mid-year reserve review will reduce rates for second half 2021. Chemical segment faced higher ethylene and energy costs offsetting price improvements while cost of purchased commodities increased with higher crude prices.
material_events: Completed $3.1 billion cash tender offers in July 2021 to retire senior notes with maturities from 2022-2026, reducing face value debt to approximately $32.0 billion. Repaid debt upon maturity during first quarter and divested non-operated DJ Basin assets while selling WES units during the period. Recorded $403 million loss contingency related to former Ecuador operations.
macro_exposures: Operations remain highly dependent on oil prices and Midland-to-Gulf-Coast spreads with continued COVID-19 pandemic impacts on demand patterns. OPEC+ agreement for phased production increases over eighteen months could pressure prices while potential resurgence of COVID-19 cases or renewed travel restrictions pose downside risks to commodity demand and pricing.
forward_outlook: Maintaining 2021 operational priorities to maximize cash flow with $2.9 billion annualized capital budget while sustaining production at Q4 2020 levels. Intends to use excess cash flow and divestiture proceeds to strengthen balance sheet through debt reduction. Zero Coupon notes totaling approximately $1.0 billion may be put to company in October 2021 while interest rate swaps require cash settlement in Q3 2021.
uncertainty_profile: Non-investment grade credit ratings may require additional collateral for contractual arrangements including pipeline transportation and environmental obligations. Interest rate swap fair values will fluctuate with market conditions through mandatory termination dates while asset divestiture timing and proceeds remain uncertain. Environmental remediation obligations continue under various federal and international laws with retroactive application.
